ground_truth.title: Discussion of our review of Apple iPad Pro Tablet (2024)
ground_truth.author: Chris Hall
ground_truth.main_content:
Chris Hall
Sep 21, 2024

Read the Apple iPad Pro Tablet (2024) Review.
Powered by hardware that you'd expect to find in a MacBook, is Apple's 2024 iPad Pro simply the best tablet there is and a replacement for your laptop?

Write your own review for Apple iPad Pro 11 inch

lgans316
Sep 21, 2024

Bang on the money except the asking price

Deleted member 621715
Sep 21, 2024

Great iPad. I thought the keyboard was OTT so bought one from China for a fraction of the price which works well enough for me

MacrosTheBlack
Sep 21, 2024

Apple make good devices and this iPad Pro would be worth the base asking price, if the base storage was 2TB.

Ed Selley
Sep 21, 2024

I've been using iPad Pros since 2019 and I currently have a 2021 M1 model with the smart keyboard. It's genuinely excellent and it means my MBP rarely has to leave the house as it can handle my standard workflow very well. I would generally be in the market to replace the '21 model about now but the decision to ensure that the higher spec processor requires me to blow £1,600 on a 1TB version and then splash an extra £300 on the keyboard makes it decidedly poor value compared to preceding years.

HMHB
Sep 21, 2024

I'm still using my M1 2021 ipad pro as well and will always stick to ipads mainly because none of the synthesizer apps I've purchased are available on Android. It's a solid piece of kit albeit not cheap!

SMeister
Sep 21, 2024

Still on my 2018 pro (gen 1 I think) with 256MB storage. When reviewed at the time they said it was overpowered - it's still as fast today as when I bought it, battery life is not as good obviously but it's been a rock for me. Good to know if I ever need to replace it I can pick this one up. Pricey yes but if it lasts as long my current one it's money well spent.

Anubis1
Sep 21, 2024

Stupid question but is the Retina display Oled or LCD?

karma12
Sep 21, 2024

still using my 10.5 pro 2017 works perfectly and just updated to IOS 17

karma12
Sep 21, 2024

Anubis1 said:
Stupid question but is the Retina display Oled or LCD?

oled i think

iamsludge
Sep 21, 2024

I'm still using the 2018 model daily, and am looking forward to getting one of these new ones primarily for the new ZBrush for iPad - which looks fantastic.

Getting the 13" model will quite simply negate me buying either a "cheap" Wacom display (for £800) or a better "pro" 17" Wacom display for £2800 (including a £449 stand) so I think the iPad Pro is really good value for my use-case as it is far more capable than the Wacom devices. Wacom prices seem to make Apple look cheap.

Jules Tohpipi
Sep 22, 2024

I'm delighted to see these specs in the new iPad Pros. Not for today but future years when they'll be an excellent secondhand purchase. I use a 12.9" Gen 3 and the family each use 2017 10.5" Pros and we're all happy and all bought used not new. I'm really looking forward to upgrading to an OLED screen in about three years time.

Jason56d
Sep 22, 2024

Anubis1 said:
Stupid question but is the Retina display Oled or LCD?

The iPad Pro comes in 13- and 11-inch sizes, sticking to the Apple's preferred 4:3 aspect ratio, with both offering a 264ppi pixel density. That comes from the 2420 x 1668 pixel resolution on the 11-inch model. This what Apple calls a Tandem OLED display, so there are two OLED layers, designed to boost the brightness. It will give you 1000 nits in normal use, with a peak of 1600 nits in HDR content and it's one of the brightest tablet displays around.

SHATNERSTACTIC
Sep 22, 2024

I upgraded to an 11 Pro from a 2017 10.5 iPad Pro and have been very happy with it. I hope to get seven years of service out of this one.

Some users have had issues with the iOS 18 update, which is unfortunate, but Apple will sort it out. In the longer term, I've read speculation that 8GB of memory will be a bottleneck for the device when Apple Intelligence is introduced (apparently, that will require 2.5GB). I suspect we'll see iPads and iPhones Pros starting with 12 or 16 GB next year.

Dimplex
Sep 22, 2024

Crazy price, some people must have more money than sense.

Kivancli
Sep 22, 2024

Dimplex said:
Crazy price, some people must have more money than sense.

Sooner or later something's gonna give ... Apple got away with it all these years cause, well, people keep throwing money at their products no matter how high the cost, but, like we have seen with the Apple Vision Pro this past year, its pretty much collecting dust ... for the mere price of $3500

The author is right. For the average Joe, the M2 Air iPad will do just fine, at almost half the price.

Joe C
Sep 23, 2024

I've got the 13" version, replacing the older 2nd gen one - it's so thin!

Has become a laptop replacement for me when travelling, with the Magic Keyboard I can access our VDI solution when needed, otherwise the M365 suite works perfectly.

Obviously this was the case with the previous one too, but this one is lighter and the battery is better - significantly outlasts the HP Dragonfly I was using beforehand. Really happy with it

JimTheBin
Sep 23, 2024

So when mentioned creative industries, what exactly does that mean or rather what industries and what you might use it for?

psikey
Sep 23, 2024

I've tried with Samsung, Microsoft (surface) and Apple many times but never found a regular use for a Tablet that a nice 13" light-weight laptop and 6"+ phone combo doesn't serve much better for that mobile & productivity combo.

Saying that, just bought an Xperia 1 VI and that was £960

The iPad looks nice as always.

Sora 66
Sep 23, 2024

I'm looking to get one for my mrs for Christmas, as she does a lot of fashion design boards and tech packs. Just a bit stuck on on if I should get the 6th gen but sacrifice on storage or go for 4th or 5th gen with more storage

Chris Hall
Sep 23, 2024

JimTheBin said:
So when mentioned creative industries, what exactly does that mean or rather what industries and what you might use it for?

Often the examples are video editing, music creation, photo work. The focus is often on demonstrating that there is enough power for working with these larger file sizes. Which is all great if that fits your workflow - for me it doesn't and the workaround involves using lots of different apps, which ends up taking a lot longer than a Mac or PC.

Sora 66
Sep 23, 2024

Chris Hall said:
Often the examples are video editing, music creation, photo work. The focus is often on demonstrating that there is enough power for working with these larger file sizes. Which is all great if that fits your workflow - for me it doesn't and the workaround involves using lots of different apps, which ends up taking a lot longer than a Mac or PC.

On average, how big are the file sizes your working with?

Chris Hall
Sep 23, 2024

Sora 66 said:
On average, how big are the file sizes your working with?

Me personally not too large - the RAW files I edit are about 30MB and the iPad has no problem with this. What I struggle with is a simple thing like cropping - although you can get "Photoshop" I can't specify the pixels for a crop, and I can't batch resize images, which slows me down a lot. Editing the image is simple enough.

Sora 66
Sep 23, 2024

Chris Hall said:
Me personally not too large - the RAW files I edit are about 30MB and the iPad has no problem with this. What I struggle with is a simple thing like cropping - although you can get "Photoshop" I can't specify the pixels for a crop, and I can't batch resize images, which slows me down a lot. Editing the image is simple enough.

Ahh I'm with you. Thinking of getting one for my Mrs as she uses her laptop with an entry level Wacom for fashion design so I feel an iPad is a decent addition not a replacement. Issue is pricing for storage included, as I think 128gb base for a pro model at close to a grand is bonkers in my eyes.

Joe C
Sep 23, 2024

SHATNERSTACTIC said:
I upgraded to an 11 Pro from a 2017 10.5 iPad Pro and have been very happy with it. I hope to get seven years of service out of this one.

Some users have had issues with the iOS 18 update, which is unfortunate, but Apple will sort it out. In the longer term, I've read speculation that 8GB of memory will be a bottleneck for the device when Apple Intelligence is introduced (apparently, that will require 2.5GB). I suspect we'll see iPads and iPhones Pros starting with 12 or 16 GB next year.

I think the 1 & 2TB models already have 16GB RAM - pricey though!

Panasonic 65" GZ1500 - Panasonic UB820 BD Player
